zh.Carmarthenshire, Wales: 英國威爾斯卡馬特史爾
zh.lifeSummary: Mrs. Iorwerth 'Amaeth" ap Rys於大約1290年出生在英國威爾斯卡馬特史爾。她和Iorwerth " Amaeth" ap Rhys至少有1個兒子。

zh-hans.Carmarthenshire, Wales: 英国威尔斯卡馬特史爾
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Mrs. Iorwerth 'Amaeth" ap Rys于大约1290年出生在英国威尔斯卡馬特史爾。她和Iorwerth " Amaeth" ap Rhys至少有1个儿子。

nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Dutch: occupational name for a cobbler.
Slovenian: nickname for a talkative person, from lapati ‘to babble’, or a topographic name from dialect lap ‘abyss’. Compare Lapp 4.

variants.name: Lapp
meanings: German, English, and Dutch: from Middle High German and Middle English lappe, Middle Dutch lap ‘cloth, patch, rag’, a metonymic occupational name for a mender of clothes or shoes, or a nickname given to someone who wore ragged clothes or garments with loose folds, flaps, or hanging sleeves, or who had fleshy folds of skin on his face, neck, or body.

German: nickname for a simple-minded person, from Middle Low German lappe ‘fool’. This surname (in any of the two possible senses; see also 1 above) is also found in France (mainly Alsace, also Lorraine).
English: nickname from Anglo-Norman French lappe ‘burdock’, perhaps for someone with a prickly character.

nameMeanings.name: Rys
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Stanislaw, Janusz, Andrzej, Beata.
Polish (Ryś), Czech, and Slovak: from Polish ryś, Czech and Slovak rys ‘lynx’, probably applied as a nickname for a nimble person. Compare Ris 6.
Dutch: variant, archaic (or Americanized) and Flemish, of Rijs, itself a variant of Ris 3.

variants.name: Ris
meanings: Swiss German: Alemannic variant of   Ries   3 or   Reis   1.
Swiss German: topographic name from Middle High German risi ‘water channel (made of stone or wood) on a mountain’ for someone who lived near such a structure.

nameMeanings.name: Mrs.
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

nameMeanings.name: Iorwerth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Traditional: from iōr‘ ‘lord’ + a mutated form of berth ‘handsome’; borne in the Mabinogi by the jealous brother of Madawg , son of Maredudd . Iorwerth came to be regarded as a Welsh equivalent of Edward , but it has no actual connection with that name.

variants.name: Yorath
meanings: Anglicized form of   Iorwerth   or, in some cases, a transferred use of the surname derived from it.

en.lifeSummary: Mrs. Iorwerth 'Amaeth" ap Rys was born about 1290, in Carmarthenshire, Wales, United Kingdom. She had at least 1 son with Iorwerth " Amaeth" ap Rhys.

fr.lifeSummary: Mrs. Iorwerth 'Amaeth" ap Rys est née environ 1290, à Carmarthenshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec Iorwerth " Amaeth" ap Rhys.

de.lifeSummary: Mrs. Iorwerth 'Amaeth" ap Rys wurde um 1290 in Carmarthenshire, Wales, United Kingdom geboren. Sie hatte mit Iorwerth " Amaeth" ap Rhys mindestens 1 Sohn.
